tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P1] Rename transport.createAgent to transport.initAgent across all callers
tasks.description:
Rename the existing transport.createAgent() method to transport.initAgent() to free the createAgent name for the new full pipeline. This is a mechanical rename across the transport interface and all adapters/callers.

The existing createAgent() writes config to an existing directory (like git init). The new naming hierarchy is:
- createAgent(opts) — full pipeline: mkdir + scaffold + optional template + register
- initAgent(path, opts) — write config to existing directory (current behavior, renamed)
- registerAgent(path) — add to mesh DB cache only (already exists)

**Files to modify:**

1. packages/shared/src/transport.ts — Rename the method in the Transport interface:
   - Change `createAgent(path: string, name?: string, description?: string, runtime?: string): Promise<AgentManifest>` to `initAgent(path: string, name?: string, description?: string, runtime?: string): Promise<AgentManifest>`

2. apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/http-transport.ts — Rename the method implementation from createAgent to initAgent. The HTTP endpoint stays the same for now (PUT /api/agents/current).

3. apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct-transport.ts — Rename createAgent to initAgent in the DirectTransport adapter.

4. apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/mesh-methods.ts — If createAgent is referenced here, rename to initAgent.

5. apps/client/src/layers/entities/agent/model/use-create-agent.ts — Rename the internal transport call from transport.createAgent to transport.initAgent. Rename hook to useInitAgent. Update the barrel export in layers/entities/agent/index.ts.

6. apps/client/src/layers/features/onboarding/ui/NoAgentsFound.tsx — Update to use renamed hook/method.

7. packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts — Rename createAgent to initAgent in createMockTransport.

8. apps/server/src/routes/agents.ts — If the route handler references createAgent internally, rename.

9. apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/agents.test.ts and agents-conventions.test.ts — Update test references.

10. apps/client/src/layers/entities/agent/__tests__/agent-hooks.test.tsx — Update test references.

Acceptance criteria:
- No remaining references to the old createAgent signature that meant 'init existing directory'
- transport.initAgent(path, name?, description?, runtime?) works identically to the old createAgent
- All existing tests pass with updated method names
- TypeScript compiles cleanly with no errors
- The name 'createAgent' is now free for the new full pipeline method
tasks.activeForm: Renaming transport.createAgent to transport.initAgent

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P1] Add config.agents.defaultDirectory and dorkosKnowledge convention toggle
tasks.description:
Add the agents configuration section to UserConfigSchema and implement the dorkosKnowledge convention toggle in the context builder.

**File: packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts**

Add 'meet-dorkbot' to ONBOARDING_STEPS:
```typescript
export const ONBOARDING_STEPS = ['meet-dorkbot', 'discovery', 'pulse', 'adapters'] as const;
```

Add agents config section to UserConfigSchema:
```typescript
export const UserConfigSchema = z.object({
  // ... existing fields ...
  agents: z
    .object({
      defaultDirectory: z.string().default('~/.dork/agents'),
    })
    .default({}),
  // ... existing fields ...
});
```

**File: apps/server/src/services/core/context-builder.ts (or wherever context building happens)**

After existing SOUL.md and NOPE.md injection, add dorkosKnowledge block:
```typescript
if (manifest.conventions?.dorkosKnowledge !== false) {
  blocks.push(buildDorkosContextBlock());
}

function buildDorkosContextBlock(): string {
  return `<dorkos_context>
DorkOS is the operating system for autonomous AI agents.
Subsystems: Console (chat), Pulse (scheduling), Relay (messaging), Mesh (discovery).
Documentation: https://dorkos.ai/llms.txt
Full docs: https://dorkos.ai/docs
</dorkos_context>`;
}
```

Default: ON for all agents. When agent.json has conventions.dorkosKnowledge: false, omit the block.

**Tests:**

In config-schema tests:
- UserConfigSchema accepts agents.defaultDirectory
- Default value is '~/.dork/agents'
- ONBOARDING_STEPS includes 'meet-dorkbot'

In context-builder tests (apps/server/src/services/core/__tests__/context-builder.test.ts or similar):
- DorkOS knowledge block injected when dorkosKnowledge: true (default)
- DorkOS knowledge block injected when conventions is undefined (default ON)
- DorkOS knowledge block omitted when dorkosKnowledge: false
- Block contains expected content (DorkOS description, subsystems, docs links)

Acceptance criteria:
- config.agents.defaultDirectory available in UserConfigSchema with default '~/.dork/agents'
- 'meet-dorkbot' added to ONBOARDING_STEPS
- Context builder injects <dorkos_context> block by default
- Context builder omits block when dorkosKnowledge: false
- All existing tests still pass
tasks.activeForm: Adding agents config and dorkosKnowledge convention toggle

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P2] Add meet-dorkbot to onboarding flow and update post-onboarding navigation
tasks.description:
Wire the 'meet-dorkbot' step into the existing onboarding system and update post-onboarding navigation to land in a chat session with DorkBot.

The onboarding flow currently goes: Welcome → Discovery → Pulse → Adapters → done. This task inserts Meet DorkBot after Welcome and changes the post-completion destination.

**Updated flow:**
```
Welcome (step -1)
  ↓ 'Get Started'
Meet DorkBot (step 0) ← NEW
  ↓ 'Create DorkBot'
Discovery (step 1)
  ↓ 'Next'
Pulse (step 2)
  ↓ 'Complete'
Chat with DorkBot ← lands here after all steps
```

**Files to modify:**

1. The onboarding orchestrator component (wherever the step flow is managed in layers/features/onboarding/) — add 'meet-dorkbot' as a step that renders the MeetDorkBotStep component (created in task 2.3). Insert it as the first step after welcome.

2. The onboarding completion handler — after all steps are marked complete, navigate to the chat session with DorkBot instead of the default landing page. Use the router to navigate to `/session?dir={dorkbotAgentPath}` or equivalent.

3. The onboarding state/config — ensure 'meet-dorkbot' is tracked in completedSteps and skippedSteps alongside existing steps.

4. The config-schema.ts change was done in task 1.3 (adding 'meet-dorkbot' to ONBOARDING_STEPS).

**Post-onboarding navigation:**
```typescript
// After last step completes:
const dorkbotPath = config.agents?.defaultDirectory 
  ? path.join(config.agents.defaultDirectory, 'dorkbot')
  : '~/.dork/agents/dorkbot';
navigate({ to: '/session', search: { dir: dorkbotPath } });
```

**Tests:**

Onboarding flow integration tests:
- 'meet-dorkbot' appears in step sequence after welcome
- Completing meet-dorkbot advances to discovery step
- Skipping meet-dorkbot marks it as skipped and advances
- After all steps complete, navigation goes to /session with DorkBot directory
- Onboarding state persists 'meet-dorkbot' in completedSteps

Acceptance criteria:
- Meet DorkBot is the first step after Welcome in onboarding
- Step is properly tracked in completedSteps/skippedSteps
- Post-onboarding navigation lands user in chat with DorkBot
- Existing onboarding steps (discovery, pulse, adapters) still work
- Step can be skipped without breaking the flow
tasks.activeForm: Adding meet-dorkbot to onboarding flow

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P2] Implement magic transition from onboarding to chat
tasks.description:
Implement the LayoutGroup + layoutId animation that morphs the personality preview bubble from MeetDorkBotStep into DorkBot's first chat message when onboarding completes.

**Implementation approach:**

Wrap both the onboarding view and the chat view in a shared LayoutGroup so Motion can animate between them:

```tsx
// In the component that switches between onboarding and chat (likely App.tsx or a wrapper)
<LayoutGroup id="onboarding-to-chat">
  {showOnboarding ? (
    <MeetDorkBotStep>
      {/* Preview bubble with layoutId */}
      <motion.div layoutId="dorkbot-first-message" className="rounded-lg border bg-muted/50 p-4">
        {previewText}
      </motion.div>
    </MeetDorkBotStep>
  ) : (
    <ChatPanel>
      {/* First message inherits layoutId */}
      <motion.div layoutId="dorkbot-first-message" className="...chat-message-styles...">
        {firstMessage}
      </motion.div>
    </ChatPanel>
  )}
</LayoutGroup>
```

Key implementation details:

1. The LayoutGroup must wrap BOTH components so Motion can animate between them. This means the parent component that decides whether to show onboarding or chat must provide the LayoutGroup.

2. The preview bubble in MeetDorkBotStep needs to accept a layoutId prop or be wrapped with one internally. The component should expose a `previewLayoutId` prop that the parent can set.

3. The ChatPanel (or the message list) needs to recognize DorkBot's first message and wrap it with the matching layoutId. This could be done by checking if the first message in the session matches the DorkBot welcome pattern.

4. The first message content comes from generateFirstMessage(traits) — this must be passed from onboarding to the chat context. Store it in the creation response or in a Zustand store so the chat panel can access it.

5. The layoutId animation only needs to fire once (on the transition from onboarding to chat). After that, the first message is a normal chat message.

**DorkBot's first message generation:**

The first message is generated server-side during creation using generateFirstMessage(traits). The creation response should include a `firstMessage` field that the client stores and uses for the initial chat display. This is NOT an LLM call — it's a pre-written template keyed by trait levels.

**Files to modify:**

1. The parent component that manages onboarding vs. chat rendering — wrap with LayoutGroup
2. MeetDorkBotStep.tsx — ensure preview bubble has layoutId="dorkbot-first-message"
3. ChatPanel or message rendering — wrap DorkBot's first message with matching layoutId
4. Creation pipeline response — include firstMessage field

**Tests:**

- LayoutGroup wraps both onboarding and chat views
- Preview bubble in MeetDorkBotStep has layoutId="dorkbot-first-message"
- First chat message after onboarding has layoutId="dorkbot-first-message"
- generateFirstMessage is called with traits from creation
- Transition from onboarding=true to onboarding=false triggers layout animation

Acceptance criteria:
- Smooth animated transition from onboarding preview bubble to first chat message
- LayoutGroup id="onboarding-to-chat" wraps the transition boundary
- Both elements share layoutId="dorkbot-first-message"
- First message content matches personality traits
- Animation is GPU-accelerated (Motion handles this via layoutId)
- Works correctly when onboarding completes and chat mounts
tasks.activeForm: Implementing magic transition from onboarding to chat

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P3] Add 'New Agent' button to AgentsHeader and 'Create Agent' to command palette
tasks.description:
Wire the CreateAgentDialog into the two primary UI entry points: the agents page header and the command palette.

**File: apps/client/src/layers/widgets/agents/ (AgentsHeader.tsx or equivalent)**

Add a 'New Agent' button next to the existing 'Scan for Agents' button:
```tsx
import { useAgentCreationStore } from '@/layers/features/agent-creation';

// In AgentsHeader component:
const openCreateDialog = useAgentCreationStore((s) => s.open);

<Button variant="default" onClick={openCreateDialog}>
  <Plus className="size-4" />
  New Agent
</Button>
```

The 'New Agent' button should be the primary action (variant="default"), positioned before or alongside 'Scan for Agents'.

**File: apps/client/src/layers/features/command-palette/ (use-palette-actions.ts or equivalent)**

Add 'Create Agent' action to the command palette actions list:
```typescript
import { useAgentCreationStore } from '@/layers/features/agent-creation';

// In the actions array:
{
  id: 'create-agent',
  title: 'Create Agent',
  description: 'Create a new agent workspace',
  icon: Plus,
  shortcut: undefined,
  handler: () => {
    closePalette();
    useAgentCreationStore.getState().open();
  },
}
```

The handler closes the palette first, then opens the creation dialog via the Zustand store's getState().open() (works outside React components).

**Tests:**

AgentsHeader tests:
- 'New Agent' button renders in header
- Clicking 'New Agent' calls useAgentCreationStore.open()

Command palette tests:
- 'Create Agent' action appears in palette results
- Selecting 'Create Agent' closes palette and opens creation dialog

Integration test (agents page):
- From agents page, click 'New Agent' → dialog opens → fill name → create → agent appears in list

Acceptance criteria:
- 'New Agent' button visible on /agents page header
- 'Create Agent' searchable and selectable in command palette
- Both entry points open the same CreateAgentDialog via Zustand store
- FSD layer imports are valid (features can import from features at same level via store)

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P4] Create default template catalog and schema
tasks.description:
Create the template catalog schema, default templates constant, and file-based user catalog stored at ~/.dork/agent-templates.json.

**File: packages/shared/src/template-catalog.ts (new)**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const TemplateCatalogSchema = z.object({
  version: z.literal(1),
  templates: z.array(
    z.object({
      id: z.string().min(1),
      name: z.string().min(1),
      description: z.string(),
      source: z.string().min(1),
      category: z.enum(['general', 'frontend', 'backend', 'library', 'tooling', 'custom']),
      builtin: z.boolean().default(false),
    })
  ),
});

export type TemplateCatalog = z.infer<typeof TemplateCatalogSchema>;
export type TemplateEntry = TemplateCatalog['templates'][number];

export const DEFAULT_TEMPLATES: TemplateEntry[] = [
  {
    id: 'blank',
    name: 'Blank Workspace',
    description: 'Empty workspace with AGENTS.md and .claude/rules/',
    source: 'github:dorkos/templates/blank',
    category: 'general',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'nextjs',
    name: 'Next.js App',
    description: 'Next.js 16 + Tailwind CSS 4 + TypeScript',
    source: 'github:ixartz/Next-js-Boilerplate',
    category: 'frontend',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'vite-react',
    name: 'Vite + React SPA',
    description: 'React 19 + Vite 6 + TanStack Router + Tailwind',
    source: 'github:RicardoValdovinos/vite-react-boilerplate',
    category: 'frontend',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'express-api',
    name: 'Express REST API',
    description: 'Express + TypeScript + Zod + Vitest + Swagger',
    source: 'github:edwinhern/express-typescript',
    category: 'backend',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'fastapi',
    name: 'FastAPI Python API',
    description: 'FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Docker + SQLModel',
    source: 'github:fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template',
    category: 'backend',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'ts-library',
    name: 'TypeScript Library',
    description: 'TypeScript + tsup + Vitest + dual ESM/CJS',
    source: 'github:jasonsturges/tsup-npm-package',
    category: 'library',
    builtin: true,
  },
  {
    id: 'cli-tool',
    name: 'Node.js CLI Tool',
    description: 'TypeScript + commander + tsup + Vitest',
    source: 'github:kucherenko/cli-typescript-starter',
    category: 'tooling',
    builtin: true,
  },
];
```

Add barrel export from packages/shared (e.g., @dorkos/shared/template-catalog).

**Tests: packages/shared/src/__tests__/template-catalog.test.ts (new)**

- TemplateCatalogSchema validates correct catalog structure
- TemplateCatalogSchema rejects missing version field
- TemplateCatalogSchema rejects invalid category values
- TemplateCatalogSchema rejects templates with empty id or name
- DEFAULT_TEMPLATES has exactly 7 entries
- DEFAULT_TEMPLATES all have builtin: true
- DEFAULT_TEMPLATES all pass TemplateCatalogSchema validation (wrap in { version: 1, templates: [...] })
- Each DEFAULT_TEMPLATES entry has unique id
- Category distribution: 1 general, 2 frontend, 2 backend, 1 library, 1 tooling

Acceptance criteria:
- TemplateCatalogSchema and TemplateEntry exported from @dorkos/shared
- DEFAULT_TEMPLATES constant with 7 built-in templates
- All templates pass schema validation
- Category enum includes 'custom' for user-added templates
- Schema enforces non-empty id, name, and source

tasks.subject: [agent-creation-and-templates] [P5] Implement DorkBot recreation in Settings
tasks.description:
Add a 'Recreate DorkBot' affordance in Settings when DorkBot doesn't exist, with a simplified personality slider step.

**Detection:**

On the Settings page (or Agents section of settings), query the mesh registry for an agent named 'dorkbot'. If not found, show the recreation button.

```typescript
// Hook or inline query
const { data: agents } = useAgents(); // existing hook that fetches agent list
const dorkbotExists = agents?.some(a => a.name === 'dorkbot');
```

**File: apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ (wherever the Agents settings section lives)**

Add a conditional 'Recreate DorkBot' section:

```tsx
{!dorkbotExists && (
  <Card className="border-dashed">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle className="text-base">DorkBot</CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>
        DorkBot is the default DorkOS agent. It was deleted or not created during onboarding.
      </CardDescription>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setShowRecreation(true)}>
        Recreate DorkBot
      </Button>
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
)}
```

**Recreation flow:**

When 'Recreate DorkBot' is clicked, show a simplified dialog or inline panel with:
- Personality sliders only (same 5 traits, same UI as MeetDorkBotStep Phase 2)
- All traits default to level 3 (Balanced)
- No name input (always 'dorkbot')
- No template picker
- 'Recreate' button calls transport.createAgent({ name: 'dorkbot', traits })
- On success: toast 'DorkBot recreated', invalidate agent queries
- On failure: toast error, allow retry

```tsx
function RecreateDorkBotDialog({ open, onOpenChange }: DialogProps) {
  const [traits, setTraits] = useState<TraitsMap>(DEFAULT_TRAITS);
  const createAgent = useCreateAgent();

  async function handleRecreate() {
    try {
      await createAgent.mutateAsync({
        name: 'dorkbot',
        traits,
        conventions: { soul: true, nope: true, dorkosKnowledge: true },
      });
      toast.success('DorkBot recreated');
      onOpenChange(false);
    } catch (err) {
      toast.error(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to recreate DorkBot');
    }
  }

  return (
    <Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={onOpenChange}>
      <DialogContent>
        <DialogHeader>
          <DialogTitle>Recreate DorkBot</DialogTitle>
          <DialogDescription>
            Configure DorkBot's personality. All settings can be changed later.
          </DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        {/* 5 trait sliders, same as PersonalitySection */}
        <DialogFooter>
          <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => onOpenChange(false)}>Cancel</Button>
          <Button onClick={handleRecreate} disabled={createAgent.isPending}>
            {createAgent.isPending ? 'Recreating...' : 'Recreate'}
          </Button>
        </DialogFooter>
      </DialogContent>
    </Dialog>
  );
}
```

Key principle: DorkBot does NOT auto-recreate if deleted. That would be presumptuous. The user made a deliberate choice; we respect it and provide a manual restoration path.

**Tests:**

- When no agent named 'dorkbot' exists, 'Recreate DorkBot' card appears in settings
- When dorkbot exists, recreation card is hidden
- Clicking 'Recreate DorkBot' opens dialog with personality sliders
- Sliders default to level 3
- 'Recreate' calls transport.createAgent with name 'dorkbot' and selected traits
- Success shows toast and closes dialog
- Failure shows error toast, dialog stays open for retry
- Agent list invalidated after successful recreation

Acceptance criteria:
- Settings shows 'Recreate DorkBot' only when dorkbot doesn't exist
- Simplified personality dialog (sliders only, no name/template)
- Creates dorkbot via same pipeline as onboarding
- Success invalidates queries and shows toast
- No auto-recreation — always manual user action
tasks.activeForm: Implementing DorkBot recreation in Settings
